leadSources.title: YC Companies
leadSources.description: Y Combinator's directory of funded startups. Filter by batch, industry, and stage.

leadSources.title: Product Hunt
leadSources.description: Discover newly launched products. Great for finding founders actively building.

leadSources.title: Crunchbase Discover
leadSources.description: Search for recently funded companies by round, industry, and location.

leadSources.title: Wellfound
leadSources.description: Startup job board with company profiles. Useful for finding lean teams hiring engineers.

leadSources.title: LinkedIn
leadSources.description: Professional network for direct founder outreach and company research.

discovery.intro: Copy the command, paste into the target platform, then apply the filters exactly.

instructions: Paste command into LinkedIn search bar
Press Enter, then click People
Apply filters: Location, Company Size, Industry
Target founders, CTOs, and heads of engineering
Send short cold outreach after qualification

commands.title: Broad founder / engineering search
commands.command: Founder OR CEO OR CTO OR "Technical Founder" OR "Head of Engineering" OR "Engineering Manager"
commands.note: Main volume search for founders and technical decision-makers.

commands.title: Technical decision makers
commands.command: CTO OR "Technical Founder" OR "Head of Engineering" OR "Engineering Manager"
commands.note: Focuses on engineering leaders most likely to evaluate technical contractors.

commands.title: Founder sweep
commands.note: Run separately to capture startup founders directly.

instructions: Use when LinkedIn search quality drops
Use to widen search beyond internal platform ranking
Use to find engineering leaders fast

commands.title: SaaS / AI founders
commands.command: site:linkedin.com/in (Founder OR CEO OR CTO) (AI OR SaaS OR software) startup

commands.title: Startup engineering leaders
commands.note: Surfaces engineering leadership profiles from startups quickly.

commands.title: Product Hunt founders
commands.note: Find founders launching on Product Hunt.

commands.title: Wellfound founders
commands.note: Find startup founders with Wellfound profiles.

commands.title: Funding news
commands.note: Surface recently funded startups from TechCrunch coverage.

instructions: Use X/Twitter when founders are posting about hiring
Use Wellfound themes to discover startup companies, then identify decision-makers

commands.title: X / Twitter founder discovery
commands.command: (founder OR cofounder OR CTO) (AI OR SaaS OR software) hiring
commands.note: Use inside X search or Google with site:x.com to find founders discussing hiring.

commands.title: Wellfound discovery themes
commands.command: AI startup | SaaS startup | developer tools | analytics | workflow automation | B2B software
commands.note: Use these themes to locate startup companies, then find founders or engineering leaders.

commands.title: Recently funded AI startup
commands.note: General discovery search for newly funded companies.

commands.title: Building in public
commands.note: Find founders actively sharing their journey.

steps: Find company domain from their website or LinkedIn
Find target name (founder, CTO, or head of engineering)
Use discovery tools to find or guess their email
Try common email patterns if tools don't return results
Verify email with NeverBounce before sending

linkedin.title: LinkedIn Outreach
instructions: Keep the message to 2-3 short lines
Reference one specific thing about their company or product
Ask a simple low-friction question
Do not pitch. Optimize for reply.
Message founders, CTOs, and heads of engineering only
linkedin.example:
Saw you're building [product].
My partner builds analytics and automation systems for operational teams.
Happy to connect.

email.title: Cold Email
instructions: Verify email before sending
Keep to 3-4 short lines maximum
Reference their product, launch, team, or hiring signal
Include one brief value statement
Ask a simple question or offer to help
Do not over-explain. Brevity wins.
email.example:
Hi - saw you're building [company].

My partner builds analytics, reporting automation, and backend systems for operations-heavy teams.

Recently built a multi-tenant reporting platform used by an energy operations company.

Happy to help if backend/data engineering support would be useful.

followUp.title: Follow-Up
instructions: First follow-up on Day 3
Final follow-up on Day 7
Keep it brief - reference the prior message lightly
Ask one simple next-step question
If they reply: respond same day, ask one discovery question, suggest a short call
followUp.example: Just circling back in case this might be useful.
